title: Generics, Constraints, Inference, and Variance · Type Forensics
summary: Build reusable selectors and repositories that preserve relationships between keys, inputs, outputs, and callbacks. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a relationship-preserving generic data toolkit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.
Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.
Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
